attractions.name: Chena Hot Springs Resort
attractions.season: Open year-round; aurora season late Aug-mid Apr
attractions.blurb: Geothermal rock lake 60mi east of Fairbanks; soak at 106F under the aurora, plus an ice museum and dogsled tours.
attractions.tip: Rock lake is 18+; kids use the indoor pool. Book the SnowCat ride up Charlie Dome to get above valley cloud.
attractions.duration_note: overnight far better than a day trip

attractions.name: Murphy Dome
attractions.season: Aurora late Aug-mid Apr; summit road plowed but rough in deep winter
attractions.blurb: Highest road-accessible dome near Fairbanks at 2,930ft, with a true 360-degree horizon above the valley fog.
attractions.tip: Last 15mi of Murphy Dome Rd is unlit and icy and the summit lot is fully exposed. No services, no cell in spots.

attractions.name: Cleary Summit
attractions.season: Aurora late Aug-mid Apr; Steese Hwy plowed year-round
attractions.blurb: Plowed hilltop pullout 20mi north of Fairbanks on the Steese; sits above the valley inversion with an open north view.
attractions.tip: Lower pullout has trees blocking north. Continue to the Skiland/Fairbanks Creek Rd turnoff for the clean horizon.

attractions.name: Borealis Basecamp
attractions.season: Open year-round; clear-dome aurora igloos best Sep-Mar
attractions.blurb: Clear-roofed fiberglass igloos on a ridge 25mi north of Fairbanks; you watch the aurora through the dome from bed.
attractions.tip: Feb-Mar books out 6-12 months ahead. Cube cabins have larger walls of glass; igloos give you the overhead dome.

attractions.name: Coldfoot Camp
attractions.season: Open year-round; only fuel and food between Yukon River and Deadhorse
attractions.blurb: Old pipeline construction camp at Dalton Mile 175, base for Brooks Range aurora, dog sledding and Atigun Pass runs.
attractions.tip: Standard trip is fly Fairbanks-Coldfoot, view aurora, then drive the Dalton back with a guide the next day.

attractions.name: Wiseman
attractions.season: Year-round; aurora peak Dec-Mar under the auroral oval
attractions.blurb: Log village of about a dozen residents at Dalton Mile 189, 60mi north of the Arctic Circle and directly under the oval.
attractions.tip: Sits in a mountain rain shadow with fewer cloudy nights than Fairbanks; Arctic Hive and Boreal Lodging take bookings.
attractions.duration_note: 2 nights to give weather a chance

attractions.name: Aurora Ice Museum
attractions.season: Open year-round; kept at 25F by geothermal chillers
attractions.cost: ~$25 tour; appletini in an ice glass extra
attractions.blurb: World's largest year-round ice structure at Chena Hot Springs: ice bar, ice bedrooms and life-size carved knights.
attractions.tip: Four scheduled tours a day and they sell out; sign up at the resort desk on arrival. Parkas are provided.
attractions.duration_note: 1hr, pairs with a hot springs soak

attractions.name: Aurora Borealis Lodge
attractions.season: Nightly aurora viewing Aug 21-Apr 21; lodging year-round
attractions.blurb: Cleary Summit lodge with floor-to-ceiling north windows that also sells a warm indoor viewing session to non-guests.
attractions.tip: The viewing session (roughly 10pm-2am, hot drinks included) is the cheap way to sit warm at 2,100ft without a room.

attractions.name: Aurora Pointe Activity Center
attractions.season: Nightly Sep-mid Apr
attractions.blurb: Heated glass-walled viewing hall on 280 private acres 15min from Fairbanks; guides, snacks, no town light spill.
attractions.tip: Capped around 60 guests and includes town pickup - the sane option if you didn't rent a studded-tire car.

attractions.name: Ester Dome
attractions.season: Road plowed but steep and glare-iced Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: 2,364ft tower-topped dome just west of Fairbanks - the closest real aurora hill to town, 25min from downtown.
attractions.tip: Henderson Rd up the north side turns to glare ice by January; without studs and 4WD use Murphy Dome instead.

attractions.name: Arctic Circle Sign, Dalton Highway
attractions.season: Dalton plowed year-round; wayside campground summer only
attractions.blurb: BLM wayside at Dalton Mile 115 with the interpretive sign at 66 degrees 33 minutes - the standard latitude photo stop.
attractions.tip: About 5hr each way from Fairbanks on gravel. Rental contracts usually forbid the Dalton - take a tour van instead.
attractions.duration_note: full day round trip from Fairbanks

attractions.name: Chena Lake Recreation Area
attractions.season: Year-round; ice houses and aurora Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: 2,000-acre borough park by North Pole; the frozen lake gives a flat dark north horizon plus rentable ice-fishing huts.
attractions.tip: The borough rents three heated ice houses by the day - lake is stocked with rainbow trout, char and silvers.

attractions.name: Creamer's Field Migratory Waterfowl Refuge
attractions.season: Trails open year-round; aurora Sep-Apr
attractions.blurb: Former dairy turned refuge inside the Fairbanks city limits; flat open fields drop the north horizon to nothing.
attractions.tip: Only works on strong Kp nights - city glow is real. Free lot on College Rd; ski and snowshoe trails are groomed.

attractions.name: UAF Geophysical Institute
attractions.season: Aurora forecast published nightly Aug 21-Apr 21
attractions.blurb: UAF West Ridge institute that publishes the free 3-day aurora forecast used by every tour operator in Alaska.
attractions.tip: Kp 2-3 is enough at Fairbanks latitude; darkness and clear sky matter far more than Kp. Stay up until 1-3am.
attractions.duration_note: check the forecast daily, not the place

attractions.name: Eagle Summit
attractions.season: Plowed but closes without warning on drifting-snow days
attractions.blurb: 3,624ft treeless pass at Steese Mile 108 - huge unobstructed sky, and a solstice midnight-sun viewpoint in June.
attractions.tip: Ground blizzards shut this pass regularly; check 511.alaska.gov before committing to the 2hr drive from Fairbanks.

attractions.name: Chatanika Lodge
attractions.season: Year-round, but hours are erratic in deep winter
attractions.blurb: Kitsch-crammed roadhouse at Steese Mile 28.5 - burgers, a bar and a parking lot that doubles as an aurora stop.
attractions.tip: It's the only warm building past Cleary Summit for 70 miles. Call ahead below -30F; the kitchen closes early.
attractions.duration_note: 1-2hr stop on a Steese aurora run

attractions.name: Iditarod Ceremonial Start
attractions.season: First Saturday in March, 10am (Mar 7 in 2026)
attractions.cost: Free to spectate; ~$1,000+ to ride in a sled as an Iditarider
attractions.blurb: Teams run an 11mi parade lap through downtown Anchorage on trucked-in snow - closest you get to 1,000 dogs at once.
attractions.tip: Stand at Cordova St or the Campbell Airstrip end for room; downtown 4th Ave is shoulder-to-shoulder by 9am.

attractions.name: Iditarod Official Restart, Willow Lake
attractions.season: Sunday after the ceremonial start, 2pm (Mar 8 in 2026)
attractions.blurb: Where the race actually starts: teams launch off frozen Willow Lake, 80mi north of Anchorage on the Parks Hwy.
attractions.tip: Parks Hwy backs up badly after 1pm - leave Anchorage by 10am. 2025's low-snow year moved it to Fairbanks; 2026 was back at Willow.

attractions.name: World Ice Art Championships
attractions.season: Mid-Feb carving through late March (extended into April in 2026)
attractions.blurb: Carvers from 30-odd countries cut Fairbanks pond ice into multi-block sculptures; now held at Tanana Valley Fairgrounds.
attractions.tip: Go after dark when the sculptures are lit; the kids' ice park has real ice slides and mazes. Dress for -20F.

attractions.name: Anchorage Fur Rendezvous
attractions.season: Late Feb-early Mar; 11 days (Feb 26-Mar 8 in 2026)
attractions.blurb: Alaska's oldest winter festival: World Championship sprint sled dog races on 4th Ave, snow sculptures, outhouse races.
attractions.tip: Running of the Reindeer is the ticketed one and sells out. Rondy overlaps the Iditarod start - stack both trips.

attractions.name: Yukon Quest Alaska 750 Start
attractions.season: Early-mid February from downtown Fairbanks
attractions.blurb: Since the international race split, Yukon Quest Alaska runs an all-Alaska loop starting and finishing in Fairbanks.
attractions.tip: 2026's first running of the new 750 (GPS-measured ~850mi) drew only six teams; check the field before planning a trip.

attractions.name: Iditarod Trail Sled Dog Race Headquarters
attractions.season: Open year-round; summer hours longer, cart rides Jun-Aug
attractions.blurb: Race HQ on Knik-Goose Bay Rd with trophies, Balto lineage exhibits, puppies to hold and a summer dog-cart ride.
attractions.tip: Easy 45min stop on the way to Denali. Puppy availability is seasonal - call ahead if that's the reason you're going.

attractions.name: Arctic Man Ski & Sno-Go Classic
attractions.season: Early-mid April at Summit Lake, Richardson Hwy Mile 195
attractions.cost: ~$100 gate pass; you supply the camp
attractions.blurb: Skier drops 1,700ft then gets towed at 85mph by a snowmachine; a 10,000-person tent city appears for four days.
attractions.tip: No lodging within 70mi - it's RV-and-snowmachine self-supported camping at -10F. 2026 marked the 40th running.

attractions.name: Nenana Ice Classic Tripod
attractions.season: Tripod planted early March, falls late Apr-early May
attractions.blurb: A wooden tripod on the frozen Tanana wired to a clock; Alaska bets on the exact minute breakup topples it.
attractions.tip: Tickets sell in Interior gas stations Feb-Apr only. Off-season the tripod and jackpot board sit by the depot.

attractions.name: Iditarod Burled Arch Finish, Nome
attractions.season: Finish week mid-March; Nome aurora Sep-Apr
attractions.blurb: Front Street finish under the burled spruce arch; teams keep trickling in for a week after the winner arrives.
attractions.tip: Winner usually lands 9-10 days after the restart. Nome's hotels sell out a year ahead for finish week.

attractions.name: Alyeska Resort
attractions.season: Lifts roughly mid-Nov-mid Apr; tram runs year-round
attractions.blurb: Alaska's only full-service ski resort, 40mi from Anchorage: 1,610 vertical metres, 650in average snow, sea-level base.
attractions.tip: Night skiing under lights Thu-Sat is the local move. Aurora is visible from the tram deck on clear Kp 4+ nights.
attractions.duration_note: day trip or overnight from Anchorage

attractions.name: Black Spruce Dog Sledding
attractions.season: Winter sled tours ~Nov-Mar; cart tours summer/fall
attractions.blurb: Kennel run by Iditarod mushers Jeff and KattiJo Deeter; private winter tours where you actually drive the team.
attractions.tip: Private-only, so it books out weeks ahead in Feb-Mar. Choose the longer tour if you want to drive, not ride.

attractions.name: Hatcher Pass & Independence Mine (winter)
attractions.season: Road plowed to the Gold Mint/mine lot; pass road over to Willow closed Oct-Jun
attractions.blurb: Alpine bowl above Palmer with a derelict 1930s gold mine; in winter it's backcountry skiing, sledding and aurora.
attractions.tip: Only the Palmer side is plowed - you cannot drive through to Willow before late June. Avalanche terrain above the lot.
attractions.duration_note: half day from Anchorage or Palmer

attractions.name: Thompson Pass
attractions.season: Heli and snowcat season roughly Feb-mid Apr
attractions.cost: $1,200+ per heli-ski day; free to just drive it
attractions.blurb: Richardson Hwy Mile 26 pass that averages ~550in of snow - the core of Alaska big-mountain heli-skiing.
attractions.tip: Even non-skiers should drive it: 30ft plowed snow walls in March. Pass closes for avalanche control without notice.
attractions.duration_note: full day; heli weeks are weather-dependent

attractions.name: Keystone Canyon Ice Climbing
attractions.season: Ice forms Dec-Mar; Valdez Ice Fest in February
attractions.blurb: Roadside frozen waterfalls in a Richardson Hwy canyon 13mi from Valdez - one of North America's densest ice-climbing zones.
attractions.tip: Bridal Veil and Horsetail are visible from the pullouts even if you don't climb. Watch for avalanche closures on the highway.
attractions.duration_note: half day; guided courses run 2 days

attractions.name: Talkeetna (winter)
attractions.season: Aurora Sep-Apr; town very quiet Nov-Feb
attractions.blurb: Only 2.5hr from Anchorage and dark enough for aurora, with Denali on the north horizon from the river confluence.
attractions.tip: Walk to the end of Main St to the Susitna confluence for the open north view. Winter rates run half of summer.

attractions.name: Denali National Park Winter Visitor Center
attractions.season: Serves as the park visitor center mid-Sep-mid-May
attractions.cost: Free in winter (no entrance fee collected)
attractions.blurb: The MSLC stands in as Denali's winter visitor center; park road is plowed only to Mile 3, then it's ski/snowshoe/mush.
attractions.tip: Park Road is still severed at Mile 43 by the Pretty Rocks landslide; the Polychrome bridge is targeted to open in 2027.
attractions.duration_note: half day; free loaner snowshoes some days

attractions.name: Turnagain Pass
attractions.season: Snowmachine and ski season roughly Dec-Apr
attractions.blurb: Seward Hwy Mile 68 pass split by law: snowmachines on the east side, skiers and split-boarders on the west.
attractions.tip: Big plowed lots on both sides. Check the Chugach Avalanche Center forecast - this pass kills people most winters.

attractions.name: Kincaid Park
attractions.season: Groomed and lit for classic and skate ski Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: 1,500 acres of lit, machine-groomed nordic trails at the west end of Anchorage, with Cook Inlet and moose throughout.
attractions.tip: Lit loops run until 10pm so you can ski after a work day. Moose on trail have right of way - back off, don't pass.

attractions.name: Eaglecrest Ski Area
attractions.season: Roughly Dec-early Apr, typically Thu-Sun operating
attractions.blurb: City-owned area on Douglas Island 30min from downtown Juneau; maritime snow, big rain risk, cheap tickets.
attractions.tip: Confirm operating days before flying in - it runs a limited weekly schedule and closes on low-snow or rain weeks.

attractions.name: Mount Aurora Skiland
attractions.season: Typically Fri-Sun and holidays, Dec-Mar
attractions.blurb: Small volunteer-flavoured ski hill on Cleary Summit, 20mi north of Fairbanks; 1,100ft vertical off one double chair.
attractions.tip: Its lodge and lot double as an aurora perch - the same summit as the Cleary pullouts but with a warm room and coffee.

attractions.name: Birch Hill Recreation Area
attractions.season: Groomed and partly lit Nov-Mar
attractions.blurb: Fairbanks' main groomed nordic venue on a hill above town - 25km of trails, some lit, hosts national-level races.
attractions.tip: Elevated above the river valley so it's often 15-20F warmer than downtown during a temperature inversion.

attractions.name: Big Lake
attractions.season: Safe ice roughly Jan-Mar; snowmachine hub all winter
attractions.cost: $25 sport fishing license; ~$150 guided ice-fish day
attractions.blurb: Mat-Su's winter playground 50mi from Anchorage: ice fishing for burbot and rainbow, plus a big snowmachine trail net.
attractions.tip: Rent a heated ice shack rather than freelancing; check ADF&G ice-thickness reports, early-season ice here is unreliable.

attractions.name: Manley Hot Springs
attractions.season: Year-round; Elliott Hwy plowed but remote in winter
attractions.blurb: End-of-the-road village 152mi from Fairbanks with concrete soaking tubs inside a private greenhouse full of grapes.
attractions.tip: Call the owners ahead - it's a household, not a resort, and the road is 100mi of unlit gravel/ice each way.
attractions.duration_note: overnight; do not do it as a winter day trip
